problem.statement: The sovereign Platform section mixes routine DSH policies with platform control concerns and displays static or unbound states instead of the live platform-control read model.
problem.currentWorkaround: Operators inspect separate source files and service endpoints and cannot trust the control-panel summary as a complete executive read model.

actors.role: Highest administrative authority with explicit platform read permissions.
permittedActions: Read audit events when separately authorized
forbiddenActions: Perform routine catalog, marketing, support, zone, SLA, HR, or partner operations
Mutate variables, feature flags, providers, rollouts, or services without the governed change workflow

actors.role: Independent reader of platform posture and audit evidence.

actions: authorize and return platform read models

surfaces.exclusionReason: The current P1 capability is explicitly read-only and the platform variable, rollout, audit, and rollback stores are not attached; no mutation claim is made.
Customer users must never access the sovereign control plane.
Partners consume effective outcomes only and must not access sovereign platform controls.
Captains consume effective outcomes only and must not access sovereign platform controls.
Field staff may not view or mutate sovereign platform controls.

outcome.statement: Authorized executives see one accurate read-only platform cockpit backed by platform-control, while routine operational domains and unsupported mutations are excluded.
outcome.baselineState: Multiple tabs are static, locally modeled, mislabeled, or mapped to routine DSH policy screens.
outcome.targetState: Every active executive tab either renders a live authorized read model or an explicit contract-required/restricted state.
outcome.observationWindow: Per request and every platform-control read refresh.

criteria: The default tab is an executive overview sourced from getPlatformRuntimeConfig.
Variables, feature flags, services, health, audit events, and change sets use generated platform-control types and live API reads.
Health and audit reads are independently permission-gated.
Routine zone, SLA, onboarding-fee, and notification configuration screens are not rendered by the Platform route.
Unsupported mutations and rollouts remain visibly blocked and no local simulation is offered.
Partial resource failures are visible without discarding successful reads.
failureStates: A static registry count is shown as a live KPI.
A routine operational screen is rendered in Platform.
A user without platform:read sees platform data.
A health or audit endpoint is called without its dedicated permission.
A failed optional read makes all successful read models disappear.
A mutation appears enabled while the backend returns CONTRACT_REQUIRED.

business: Platform is a sovereign executive control plane, not a daily operations workspace.
Administration owns role assignment; Platform consumes permissions.
WLT remains the only owner of financial truth.
Provider secrets remain backend-only and masked.
Every displayed state comes from platform-control or is labeled unavailable, restricted, or contract-required.
negative: No customer, partner, captain, or field surface exposes Platform controls.
No local React state, fixture, seed, fallback, or registry constant is presented as live platform truth.
No platform mutation bypasses proposal, validation, independent approval, apply, audit, readback, and rollback.
No zone, SLA, catalog, marketing, support, HR, or partner routine operation is owned by Platform.
No health or audit data is fetched without the matching dedicated permission.

execution.appetite: Bounded corrective implementation of the existing read-only platform-control capability.
fixedConstraints: Use GitHub Remote branch bassam only.
Do not create a second platform truth source.
Maximum three committed files for this execution.
forbiddenScope: Database-backed mutation workflow without migrations and independent approvals
Local demo mutation or fake runtime data
unknowns: Live runtime and visual evidence in the operator environment
